title: 清洁女工之死
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\209-清洁女工之死-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Superintendent Spence informs Hercule Poirot that the investigation into the death of Mrs. McKinty is being closed because Spence has been reassigned to a corruption case in Scotland. Spence admits he has found no evidence to suggest anyone other than the lodger, James Bentley, killed her, but he suggests Poirot take over the case because he believes the murder was not a suicide and that Poirot's methods might uncover what he missed. Poirot accepts the challenge, motivated by boredom and a desire to help Spence find peace of mind.

Spence provides case details regarding Mrs. McKinty, a sixty-four-year-old widow living in Broadhurst. She had £200 in a bank account and an additional £30 hidden under a loose floorboard in her bedroom, a fact known to her niece and James Bentley. The niece and her husband, a painter, are considered suspects but have no apparent motive or need for the money. 

James Bentley, a well-educated but struggling man, had been renting a room from Mrs. McKinty. He had fallen behind on his rent and was nearly out of money. On the night of the murder, November 22nd, Bentley claimed to have been out for a walk from 7:15 p.m. to 9:00 p.m. Mrs. McKinty died between 8:30 and 9:00 p.m. after being struck on the back of the head with a sharp object, possibly a butcher's knife. Although the windows were locked from the inside and there were no signs of forced entry, the bedroom floorboard had been lifted and the £30 was missing. The body was discovered the following morning by Mrs. Elliott after Bentley and a baker knocked on the door.
